title: Bitter Flaky Wheat Pilaf
description: A comforting Chinese (Fujian) dish featuring white wine and paleo-friendly wheat that is both bitter and flaky in texture.

ingredients.name: Wheat Vermicelli
ingredients.notes: Use gluten-free wheat vermicelli if available.

ingredients.name: Coconut Milk Powder
ingredients.notes: Optional for extra creaminess, not essential for paleo-friendly version.

ingredients.name: Bitter Melon Slices
ingredients.notes: Use the innermost and softer part of the bitter melon.

instructions: Heat water in a saucepan until boiling.
Add wheat vermicelli to the boiling water. Cook for about 2 minutes or until softened but still slightly firm.
Drain the vermicelli and set aside.
In another pan, melt coconut milk powder with white wine over low heat.
Gently fold in drained wheat vermicelli.
Add bitter melon slices to the mixture. Cook for about 2 more minutes or until the melon releases a pleasant aroma.
Remove from heat and serve hot as a side dish or dessert.
